9.reason: [gets() is unsafe and deprecated; use fgets() instead]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [gets() is unsafe and deprecated; use fgets() instead]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [gets() is unsafe and deprecated; use fgets() instead]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  gets() is deprecated and removed in C11 standard, which causes compilation errors; use scanf("%s", s) or fgets() instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  gets() is deprecated/removed in C11 standard and often causes compilation errors; use scanf("%s", s) or fgets() instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it can cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments, 'gets' is not available, which may lead to compilation errors or linker failures.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; using it may cause compilation errors or warnings with modern compilers. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Double equals used instead of assignment; should be '=' not '=='
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  error: should be assignment '=' not comparison '=='
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Uses comparison operator '==' instead of assignment operator '=', should be res[1] = '.';
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Uses comparison operator == instead of assignment =, should be res[1]='.';
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Syntax error: uses '==' (comparison) instead of '=' (assignment). Also, trailing comma is invalid syntax.]

29.reason: Missing null terminator in res; res[l] should be set to '\0' before printing

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing null terminator in res; res[l] should be set to '\0' before printing
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  missing null terminator for res
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  res array is not null-terminated, causing undefined behavior when printed; should set res[l] = '\0'; before printing.

61.reason: [If the input is like "0.001", then after extracting '1', res[1] was assigned using '==' (comparison) instead of '=' (assignment), so res[1] remains uninitialized. Also, if k == strlen(s)-1 (i.e., only one non-zero digit at end), then res[1] is never set and fputs prints garbage beyond res[0].]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing null terminator; res[l] should be '\0' after the loop
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  missing null terminator for res (and res[1] may be uninitialized)
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  res array is not null-terminated, causing undefined behavior when printed; should set res[l] = '\0'; before printing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [If the input is like "0.001", then after extracting '1', res[1] was assigned using '==' (comparison) instead of '=' (assignment), so res[1] remains uninitialized. Also, if k == strlen(s)-1 (i.e., only one non-zero digit at end), then res[1] is never set and fputs prints garbage beyond res[0].]
